steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The student claims that IGF-1 gene variations "must account for" size differences in dogs — implying it is the sole explanation. The question asks which choice most directly undermines that conclusion.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "The gene that regulates IGF-1 production is one of multiple genes known to affect the size of dogs.": directly undermines "must account for" — if other genes also contribute, one gene alone cannot explain all size variation. ✓
  • "The researchers' conclusions regarding the IGF-1 gene may not apply to other species given that dogs vary in size more than any other group of land mammals does.": addresses generalizability beyond dogs, which is irrelevant to the student's claim about dogs specifically. ✗
  • "Variations of the IGF-1 gene result in substantial body size variance among dog breeds and may influence additional characteristics as well.": supports the student's conclusion rather than undermining it. ✗
  • "In fact, only one-third of the most prevalent dog breeds carry the same variant of the gene regulating IGF-1 production.": discusses how common a variant is, not whether IGF-1 alone explains size differences. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
